title: Summer Pockets: Sorakado Ao-hen
titleNative: Summer Pockets 空門蒼編
synopsis: Theatrical edited version of Summer Pockets following Ao Sorakado's storyline from the anime series. While Hairi becomes close to Ao, a girl from the island who always seems sleepy for some reason, he witnesses Ao carrying out a "duty" that has been passed down through the generations of the Sorakado family. While helping with this "duty", Hairi learns the secret of the "butterfly" and Ao's true purpose. (Source: TOHO Cinemas, translated)

title: Fukuyama Gekijou: Natsu no Himitsu

synopsis: Fukuyama Gekijou is an anthology of short stories and animated music videos. It uses a framing device of a mouse family looking at bedtime stories to prepare for sleep.

title: The Configurators
titleNative: 锋速战警
synopsis: During ancient times, the hostile jellyfish-like aliens invaded Earth. One of the genius ape-man possessed the high-technology Dinosaur Configurator and defeated the aliens and saved the world. In present days, Configuration Technology has changed the entire world. Andy, Toothy and Hero have to defend together with the Super Force the world from the criminal scientist "The Mole" and the Space Jellyfish leader that invaded Earth millions years ago. The Space Jellyfish leader is trapped on Earth for many years and hopes to gain back its energy to conquer Earth again. Andy and his friends need to train and defend the Earth with their hi-tech robots -- The Configurators! (Source: CHINAnimax)

title: Jewelpet: Attack Travel!
titleNative: ジュエルペット あたっくとらべる！
synopsis: Ruby, Garnet, and the other familiar, jewel-eyed Jewelpet characters are suddenly going on a school trip with the teacher Iruka to China's Sichuan Province. However, there is something odd about the tour conductor, driver, and guide on their sightseeing bus as it heads towards its true destination. (Source: Anime News Network)

title: RYO
titleNative: 龍-RYO-
synopsis: Ryo is about the son of a samurai who lost his parents during the attack of English ships on a Japanese town who is given the name Ryo and becomes the bodyguard of Ryoma Sakamoto, a man who has his own plan to fight for the country. (Source: Anime News Network) Note: Ryo was one of the four films of Anime Mirai 2013 (Young Animator Training Project), a project funded by the Japanese government's Agency of Cultural Affairs in order to support training animators.

title: Catman Series III
titleNative: キャットマン
synopsis: In a city filled with anthropomorphic cat-people, Catman lives free of rules and boundaries, doing everything he wants, which often collides with the other iinhabitants of the city. Created entirely in flash, with no spoken dialog, Catman instead speaks in English and Japanese subtitles. The series is spread across three so-called 'seasons', with only the third having been aired.

title: Akuma-kun
titleNative: 悪魔くん
synopsis: The age of the demons has begun. Dr Faust has foreseen this rise of evil. Unfortunately, he is near death and is unable to personally battle this upcoming threat. Faust entrusts a young boy, Shingo Yamada, to take the responsibility of ridding the Earth of this new evil presence. Faust finds a birthmark on Shingo's forehead that signifies that he is the chosen demon fighter. Faust summons from hell what may be humanity's only hope of surviving: a less than enthusiastic devil named Mephisto rises. After signing a pact in blood to save humankind, Shingo and Mephisto set out to battle the supernatural world. (Source: AniDB)

title: Tistou Midori no Oyayubi
titleNative: チスト みどりのおやゆび
synopsis: Based on the children’s novel " Tistou les pouces verts " by French author Maurice Druon. An 8 year old boy discovers he has an unusual gift, the ability to cause anything he touches with his thumbs to grow into beautiful plants. The story follows Tistou as he attempts to use his gift to make a better world.
